findings.title: Account with empty password: bob
findings.description: Account 'bob' has an empty password hash and an interactive shell - login is possible without credentials.

findings.title: Direct root SSH login from 198.51.100.99
findings.description: Successful interactive root SSH login from 198.51.100.99 via publickey. Direct root logins should be disabled (PermitRootLogin no).
evidence: Sep 12 05:01:00 web01 sshd[22000]: Accepted publickey for root from 198.51.100.99 port 51000 ssh2: RSA SHA256:XXXXXX

findings.title: Non-root account with UID 0: toor
findings.description: Account 'toor' has UID 0 - it has full root privileges. Legitimate systems have exactly one UID 0 account named 'root'.

findings.title: Service account 'backup' has interactive shell
findings.description: The system account 'backup' is configured with an interactive shell (/bin/bash). System accounts should have /usr/sbin/nologin or /bin/false.

findings.title: Service account 'www-data' has interactive shell
findings.description: The system account 'www-data' is configured with an interactive shell (/bin/bash). System accounts should have /usr/sbin/nologin or /bin/false.

findings.title: Kernel anomaly: ANOM_PROMISCUOUS
findings.description: auditd recorded a kernel anomaly event (ANOM_PROMISCUOUS). These typically indicate crashes, abends, or promiscuous-mode interface changes.
evidence: type=ANOM_PROMISCUOUS msg=audit(1726113300.000:400): dev=eth0 prom=256 old_prom=0 auid=0 uid=0 gid=0 ses=4

findings.title: SSH brute-force pattern from 192.0.2.55
findings.description: 26 failed SSH authentications observed from 192.0.2.55 targeting 21 username(s).
evidence: users: admin, backup, demo, deploy, developer, docker, ftp, git, guest, info

findings.title: Successful SSH login after 26 failures from 192.0.2.55
findings.description: User alice authenticated successfully from 192.0.2.55 after 26 failed attempts in the prior 10 minutes.

findings.title: SELinux denial: sshd
findings.description: SELinux blocked process sshd (scontext=system_u:system_r:sshd_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023) accessing target context unconfined_u:object_r:user_home_t:s0.
evidence: type=AVC msg=audit(1726113200.111:300): avc:  denied  { execute } for  pid=2222 comm="sshd" name="badbin" dev="dm-0" ino=12345 scontext=system_u:system_r:sshd_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 tcontext=unconfined_u:object_r:user_home_t:s0 tclass=file

findings.title: PermitRootLogin = yes
findings.description: sshd is configured to allow root login. Industry best practice is 'no'. 'without-password' permits root via key.

findings.title: Root password SSH login enabled
findings.description: Both PermitRootLogin and PasswordAuthentication permit interactive root password authentication.

findings.title: USB mass-storage attach
findings.description: USB mass-storage device was attached. On servers this is anomalous and may indicate physical tampering or data exfiltration.
evidence: Sep 12 03:14:02 web01 kernel: usb-storage 1-1:1.0: USB Mass Storage device detected

findings.title: USB mass-storage attach

findings.title: AppArmor denial in syslog
findings.description: AppArmor denied an action - review the offending profile and the process being confined.
evidence: Sep 12 05:01:00 web01 kernel: audit: type=1400 audit(1726113660.123:201): apparmor="DENIED" operation="open" profile="/usr/sbin/nginx"

findings.title: SELinux denial in syslog
findings.description: SELinux denied an action. Investigate whether the denial reflects mis-configuration or actual attempted boundary violation.
evidence: Sep 12 04:00:00 web01 kernel: [ 5000.000] audit: type=1400 audit(1726113600.000:300): avc:  denied  { execute } for  pid=999 comm="bash"

findings.title: Sudoers grants www-data permission to modify sudoers
findings.description: www-data can modify the sudoers file itself - this is a complete privilege-escalation primitive.

findings.title: systemd unit sysmaint.service runs binary from world-writable location
findings.description: ExecStart for unit 'sysmaint.service' references a path in /tmp, /var/tmp or /dev/shm. Legitimate services should live under /usr/bin, /usr/local/bin, /opt, etc.

findings.title: NOPASSWD: ALL granted to alice
findings.description: alice can run any command as ALL without a password.
